name: Hafte Tir Bombing
description: On 28 June 1981 (7 Tir 1360 in the Iranian calendar), a bomb exploded at the headquarters of the [Islamic Republican Party](/orgs/islamic-republican-party) in Tehran during a leadership meeting, killing over 70 people including [Mohammad Beheshti](/people/mohammad-beheshti), four cabinet ministers, 27 members of parliament, and other senior officials. It was one of the deadliest political assassinations in modern Iranian history. The attack is generally attributed to the [Mojahedin-e Khalq](/orgs/mojahedin-e-khalq) (MEK), though the group has denied responsibility and attribution remains disputed by some analysts.
body: The bombing occurred eight days after the [June 20 uprising](/events/june-20-uprising-1981) and the beginning of the open armed conflict between the MEK and the Islamic Republic. Beheshti, widely considered the second most powerful man in Iran after [Ruhollah Khomeini](/people/ruhollah-khomeini), was the most prominent victim. The attack accelerated the regime's mass crackdown on opposition groups, particularly the MEK, and led to a period of intense political violence throughout the summer of 1981. [Mohammad-Javad Bahonar](/people/mohammad-javad-bahonar), who succeeded Beheshti as the IRP's effective leader, was himself assassinated two months later on 30 August 1981.
editorial_notes: Known as the Hafte Tir bombing (7 Tir explosion). The date is commemorated annually in Iran as Judiciary Day.
